perturbation.original_text: In addition, either Party may terminate this Agreement effective upon written notice stating its intention to terminate in the event the other Party (i) ceases to function as a going concern or to conduct operations in the normal course of business, or (ii) has a petition filed by or against it under any state or federal bankruptcy or insolvency law which petition has not been dismissed or set aside within sixty (60) days of its filing.
perturbation.changed_text: In addition, either Party may terminate this Agreement effective upon written notice stating its intention to terminate in the event the other Party (i) ceases to function as a going concern or to conduct operations in the normal course of business, or (ii) has a petition filed by or against it under any state or federal bankruptcy or insolvency law which petition has not been dismissed or set aside within thirty (30) days of its filing.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation modifies the timeframe in which a bankruptcy petition must be dismissed for termination rights to not apply. The original text specifies 'sixty (60) days', whereas the changed text specifies 'thirty (30) days'. This creates an in-text contradiction since different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines. Which terms should be enforced, leading to disputes over contractual obligations, if iVillage should terminate the agreement after 30 or 60 days of petition filing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Termination. In the event of a material breach by either Party of any term of this Agreement, the non-breaching Party may terminate this Agreement by written notice to the breaching Party if the breaching Party fails to cure such material breach within thirty (30) days of receipt of written notice thereof. In addition, either Party may terminate this Agreement effective upon written notice stating its intention to terminate in the event the other Party (i) ceases to function as a going concern or to conduct operations in the normal course of business, or (ii) has a petition filed by or against it under any state or federal bankruptcy or insolvency law which petition has not been dismissed or set aside within thirty (30) days of its filing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the timeframe for dismissing a bankruptcy petition from sixty (60) days to thirty (30) days, creating a direct conflict within the contract. The contradicted text, which includes the modified text, now states two different cure periods which contradict the original intent. This inconsistency leads to uncertainty and potential disputes regarding termination rights, as it's unclear which timeframe (30 or 60 days) should be enforced.
